lines.target: David and Joan decided to go* for a picnic in Richmond.
lines.base: David y Joan decidieron ir de campo a Richmond.
lines.pronunciation: .. richmond. 2... lain... — 3 ... pripeerin... baaskét... — 5 ... haard-boild egs... soosidchis... letis. — 6 ... louf ovbred... naivs ... foorks. — 7... miinuail... girédch. — 8 ... bushi paark. — 9 ... stopt... uain. — 10 ... hiudch... autskérts... — 11 ... kualét pleis... rag. — 12 ... soolt asuel. — 14 idiót!... apsel... — 16 ... kookskru!

lines.target: On Sundays, David has a lie-in and Joan brings* him breakfast in bed.
lines.base: Los domingos David se queda acostado y Joan le leva el desayuno a la cama.

lines.target: This Sunday, David got* up at eleven o'clock and after shaving and washing, went downstairs.
lines.base: Este domingo, David se levantó a las once y tras afeitarse y laverse, bajó.

lines.target: Joan was preparing the picnic basket.
lines.base: Joan estaba preparando una cesta de merienda.

lines.target: She put* in cold meat, hard boiled eggs, some cold sausages and a lettuce.
lines.base: Puso (dentro) carne fría, huevos duros, salchichas frías y lechuga.

lines.target: She added a loaf of bread, some butter and the knives and forks.
lines.base: Añadió una barra de pan, mantequilla y los cuchillos y tenedores.

lines.target: Meanwhile David got” the car out of the garage.
lines.base: Mientras, David sacó el coche del garaje.

lines.target: They put* the basket in the boot and set” off for Bushey Park.
lines.base: Pusieron la cesta en el portamaletas y se dirigieron a Bushey Park.

lines.target: On the way, David stopped to buy some wine.
lines.base: Por el camino, David paró para comprar vino.

lines.target: Bushey Park is a huge park on the outskirts of London.
lines.base: Bushey Park es un parque inmenso en los alrededores de Londres.

lines.target: When they arrived, they found* a quiet place and sat* down on a rug to eat their lunch.
lines.base: Cuando liegaron, encontraron un lugar tranquilo y se sentaron en una manta para comer.

lines.target: Pass me some chicken, please... and some salt, as well.
lines.base: Pásame un poco de pollo, por favor y la sal también.

lines.target: Oh dear, said* Joan, | think* I've forgotten* the salt.
lines.base: ¡Vaya!, dijo Joan, creo que me he olvidado la sal.

lines.target: Idiot! said* David, but seeing” she was upset, he said:
lines.base: ¡idiota! dijo David, pero viendo que estaba molesta, dijo:

lines.target: Never mind. Let's have a glass of wine.
lines.base: No importa. Tomemos un vaso de vino.

lines.target: He took* the bottle out of the basket, then said*: “Oh dear, I've forgotten the cork-screw!”
lines.base: Cogió la botella de la cesta, y dijo: « Vaya, he olvidado el sacacorchos | ».

gap_candidates.target: David and Joan decided to go* for a picnic in Richmond.
On Sundays, David has a lie-in and Joan brings* him breakfast in bed. (1)
This Sunday, David got* up at eleven o'clock and after shaving and washing, went downstairs.
Joan was preparing the picnic basket.
She put* in cold meat, hard boiled eggs, some cold sausages and a lettuce.
She added a loaf of bread, some butter and the knives and forks.
They put* the basket in the boot and set” off for Bushey Park.
On the way, David stopped to buy some wine.
When they arrived, they found* a quiet place and sat* down on a rug to eat their lunch. (3)
Pass me some chicken, please... and some salt, as well.
Oh dear, said* Joan, | think* I've forgotten* the salt.
Idiot! said* David, but seeing” she was upset, he said: (4)
Never mind. Let's have a glass of wine.
